title: poinsettia geniculata
intro: Poinsettia geniculata is a type of flowering plant native to Mexico, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its ability to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, Poinsettia geniculata is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its purported medicinal properties.
